Null Spike.chosen_rationale: Using the mean of existing loan_amount values for imputation is a widely accepted practice that preserves data distribution.
Null Spike.chosen_caveats: This method assumes that missing values are not significantly different from other data points, which may not always hold true.

Null Spike.chosen_rationale: Using the mean of existing employment_length values is a standard practice that can help preserve the distribution of the data.
Null Spike.chosen_caveats: This method assumes that missing values are similar to existing values, which may not always hold true.

Statistical Outlier.chosen_rationale: This approach constrains extreme values to a realistic range, preserving overall data integrity.
Statistical Outlier.chosen_caveats: Assumes that values outside of the 0-1 range are indeed erroneous and should be capped.

Format Inconsistency.chosen_rationale: This approach uses a uniform date format which is widely accepted and reduces future inconsistencies.
Format Inconsistency.chosen_caveats: This may result in data loss if any dates are invalid or cannot be parsed properly.
Format Inconsistency.detail_summary: Mixed formats: 7 rows as YYYY-MM-DD, 3 rows as MM/DD/YYYY
